element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Player learns left/right movement and basic punch timing
Each opponent presents varied difficulty and fighting style within single season
Timing-based high-risk, high-reward punch technique throughout championship
Uses edited/palette-swapped Detroit Kid graphics; available as roster option

rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Turn-based engagement with emphasis on reactive timing; tension peaks around Final Blow window, releases on successful strike or opponent KO

difficulty_curve.early_game: Progressive opponent difficulty through tournament bracket; initial fighters establish baseline skill checks
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Opponent variety increases with named fictional boxers (Dynamite Joe, Fernando Gomez, Kim Nang, King Jason) presenting varied challenge profiles
difficulty_curve.late_game: Championship culmination against strongest available opponents in tournament structure
notable_spikes: Transitions between tournament rounds introduce fighter variety
Final Blow timing mastery requirement creates skill gate throughout

notable_level_patterns.description: Game structured as continuous boxing season with five fictional opponents competed sequentially; player progresses through tournament bracket format rather than discrete level progression
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Championship rounds against Dynamite Joe (Miracle Fighter), Fernando Gomez (South American Eagle), Kim Nang (Korean Comet), King Jason (Black King/Detroit Kid/Invincible Black Panther)

notable_level_patterns.description: Screen-sized boxer moves left/right within bounded arena space; opponent occupies same horizontal plane; spatial interaction limited to horizontal positioning and timing-based punch execution
Home versions include optional spectator mode allowing watch of boxer matchups without direct player control; shifts from active participation to passive observation
